title: AI Literacy Lesson 1
content: This lesson covers AI Literacy. We explore core principles driving success. The DOM is a programming interface for web documents. It represents the page so programs can change structure, style, and content. The DOM represents nodes and objects; programming languages can connect to the page. A Web page can be displayed in the browser or as HTML source. It is the same document but the DOM representation allows manipulation. As an object-oriented representation, it can be modified with JavaScript. Understanding the DOM tree structure—where every element is a node—is fundamental. Parent, child, and sibling nodes form the architecture. By using methods like appendChild, developers dynamically update the user interface without a full page reload.
